programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A charity chairman dies mysteriously, and the prime suspect is then killed at the funeral. The investigation leads to hospital administrator Norman Briggs - who could either be the guilty party or the next victim. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A womanising patient disappears and Dr Sloan finds himself trying to untangle an ambitious murder plot. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble learns that upholsterer Josef Korak, head of the refugee family with whom he lives, is also a doctor on the run

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A murder in a booby-trapped elevator leads Perry Mason to set up an elaborate electrical device in the courtroom

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The head of a crime ring is defended by Alex's estranged father. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A model collapses and dies on the catwalk during a fashion show - and it is not long before a second body is found in the cargo area just outside the venue. Meanwhile, a terminally ill patient claims he killed his doctor - but Stella and Flack realise his story cannot be true. Drama, starring Melina Kanakaredes, Eddie Cahill and Hill Harper

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Four seemingly unrelated individuals are killed in a controlled bombing at a Pasadena restaurant, but as the NCIS officers dig deeper, they discover a link between the victims - all were members of an experimental strategy game developed by the Office of Naval Intelligence. The question is, who wanted them dead - and why? Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: After Richard Kimble saves his life, thoroughbred farm owner and ex-underworld figure Arthur Brame vows that whatever problems Kimble has had are over

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason defends a doctor who finds himself accused of malpractice as well as murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee is sent to a women's prison to interview a murder suspect, but ends up being captured by the inmates, who have run riot and seized control of a cell block, afraid they will be blamed for killing a guard. Held hostage as a bargaining chip, he tries to identify the real culprit before the warden takes drastic action to resolve the crisis

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman tries to eradicate drugs from the street. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A hit-and-run driver kills a man working on the campaign for an aspiring senator, but it soon becomes a case of national security when closer examination of the video footage makes it clear he was murdered. The agents' job is to figure out whether it is an isolated incident or part of a politically motivated plot that might put others in danger - and most of the pressure is on Kensi, who takes the victim's old job and goes undercover in the campaign office

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky is attacked by a knife-wielding woman who accuses him of war crimes, alleging he tortured and killed her brother in Afghanistan in the early 1980s. Gibbs investigates and ascertains what really happened - but has to work with the Afghan embassy to avoid an international incident. Drama, starring David McCallum

LL Cool J, Chris O'Donnell, Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen star

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: Part one of two. When a doctor testifying against him in a malpractice suit is poisoned, Jesse is arrested and charged with murder, so Dr Sloan persuades old friend and attorney Ben Matlock to defend his colleague. Andy Griffith guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ben Matlock finds a trail of evidence that suggests the killers have struck before, framing more than one innocent person for murder, and sets out to catch them this time round. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Andy Griffith

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Old friends Dr Sloan and private investigator Joe Mannix are thrown together to unravel a case of corruption linked to an unsolved murder. Guest starring Mike Connors as the veteran TV detective

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team works with Toby's nemesis and his wife - Toby's ex-fiancée - to test an artificially intelligent doomsday bunker, but they soon find themselves trapped inside.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: When a solar storm knocks out the power in LA, Team Scorpion must help a teen pilot and his girlfriend who are lost over the Pacific with no radio contact.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team works with Toby's nemesis and his wife - Toby's ex-fiancée - to test an artificially intelligent doomsday bunker, but they soon find themselves trapped inside.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.desc: The locals are preoccupied with personal problems as Sally worries about her marriage, and Harding fears he is being undermined both at the factory and at home. Anna and David suspect Simon Goddard's activities in the town are not all they seem and a friendship is threatened by a former soldier's return. Rural drama, starring Thomas Craig, Samantha Giles and Brian Capron

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Danny and Kim vow to keep their romance secret, Sally helps emphysema sufferer Jack turn his life around and Nathan's leaving party is disrupted when Megan goes into labour. However, the men celebrate the new arrival in the Skelthwaite Arms, but a power cut leads to a disastrous start to the baby's life

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The townsfolk are still coming to terms with Sally's death, while Danny and Cady move into Kim's house to make space for the Boothes - leading to a declaration of love. Cathy Saunders' adoption application places Anna on the horns of a dilemma when she realises her friend is addicted to painkillers, and the factory helps out with the children's art fair - albeit in an unusual way

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Obadiah Hakeswill returns to create more mischief, kidnapping a beautiful aristocrat and undermining the discipline of Wellesley's men. Sharpe is dispatched to thwart his old foe, and manages to inflict a humiliating defeat on French forces in the process - but only at terrible personal loss to himself. Sean Bean, Elizabeth Hurley and Pete Postlethwaite star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl may be in the clear when a policeman is revealed to be less than honest. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to cure a patchy lawn at a select language school hiding dark secrets. The disappearance of a Polish student alerts them to a tale of passion, rivalry and revenge, and when a handyman is found with his throat cut they race to find the killer. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary investigate the mysterious death of a blind professor caught up in a bitter battle to save his beloved allotment from an unscrupulous land developer. A heady blend of weedkiller, cannabis and high finance hangs in the air as the duo search for clues - and another body is turned up before they track down the villain. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura are employed to restore a country churchyard in time for a medieval fair, but it is not long before a local landowner is killed by an arrow beneath an ancient yew in the centre of the graveyard. The duo investigate the possibility that rival bidders for the victim's highly desirable farm are to blame - though affairs of the heart might offer a more likely motive. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer, after his body is found by two local fishermen. As he uncovers a family's tragedies and secrets, he also learns that Brokenwood is a place where shadows lurk just beneath the surface. Drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate following series of deadly 'accidents' on a psychic TV programme hosted by two warring sisters. Crime drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: Ken Stott stars as Ian Rankin's Edinburgh detective. Rebus tries to discover the significance of an intriguing clue left at the scene of an eminent doctor's murder - a small coffin containing an effigy of a baby. A subsequent killing suggests parallels with an unsolved case, and the investigation gradually focuses on one of the city's wealthiest families. Crime thriller, with Claire Price

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: When a man dies at a cash machine and two teenage girls confess to the murder of a taxi driver, the detective appears to have two open-and-shut cases. However, Wallander's investigation leads him to discover a conspiracy that stretches far beyond the borders of Sweden. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart and David Warner

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boy's sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffrey's son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that's hanging on the wall

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford dig into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of property developments in Plymouth, Treherbert in Wales and Hinckley in Leicestershire that all turn out to take much longer than planned, threatening to prevent their new owners making money

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a senior manager gambling with an NHS credit card, and an exotic dance troupe boss goes missing after defrauding the public purse of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Scottish cyclist on a round the world challenge is hit by a driver in Texas, and a family dog saves the day when a woman slips and falls on the stairs. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets shivers down his spine when he gets to find out more about some spooky antiques. Later, he and the contenders head to the auction in Derby

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Ed, Sheila, Ciara and Jas vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. It's an emotional brief in Cheddar, as the buyers want something uplifting for their home after a recent cancer diagnosis. With all three of the arty trio working in bold and bright colours, will it be an abstract artist, an intuitive painter or a stained-glass designer who brings smiles of joy to the homeowners?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton visits rural Shropshire with a couple who want their £500,000 budget to afford them and their young children a life in the outdoors with community close by. While in the county, Alistair visits the mansion of Attingham Park to see the recent restoration successes for himself, as well as discovering some intriguing stories from its past

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott head to the south coast of England for the final leg of their challenge, where Irina finds out about the history of beach huts while in Bournemouth, Dorset. Irita also fancies a table she knows Tim will hate, while Tim purchases a fine Chinese vase. However, the clock is ticking - the world's oldest clock at Salisbury Cathedral - which chimes for Tim

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Carters attend court, where a nervous Mick prepares to read his victim statement - unaware of the drama to unfold. Sharon tells an unbelieving Zack he has to apologise to Martin, and as Tiffany sings Rocky's praises, Sonia remains unconvinced - until she is given food for thought from an unlikely source

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Richard Ayoade is the guest host, with broadcaster Richard Osman and peer Sayeeda Warsi joining regular team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the week's news headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Alan gets the chance to showcase the harder edge of his broadcasting chops by delivering an expose of the chemsex phenomenon and drama therapist Izzy Barnes leads a live workshop in the This Time studio. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Cherish Finden plus special guest Sarah Hadland. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip through Scotland, joining professional forager Gary Goldie at Airds Bay in Argyll and Bute to pick hogweed and sea arrowgrass to use as flavourings for a wild curry. She then heads to the Cairngorms National Park to visits the Blair Atholl watermill run by baker Rami Cohen, grinding her own flour to bake Indian five-spice soda bread, served with a red lentil dish. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a vegetarian haggis tarte Tatin

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: Swimming: European Championships
programme.desc: A round-up of the action so far at Danube Arena in Budapest, featuring British stars including Ben Proud, Molly Renshaw and Adam Peaty

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore's recycling mission takes her to Bredbury, Greater Manchester, where she finds a haul of pine doors, old rope and glass demijohns to convert into new, exciting objects

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers meet a couple in Clapham juggling older parents, twin babies and busy careers, while in Shrewsbury, a garden has nothing exciting for children

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Blue Peter presenters from different eras of the long-running show. Ayo Akinwolere, Mark Curry, Zoe Salmon, Simon Thomas, Mwaka Mudenda, Richie Driss, Katy Hill and Tim Vincent try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Alexandra Burke, as they assist two brothers from Middlesbrough in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a scientist, a prison officer and an electrician

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: After a year like no other, which saw the world-famous competition cancelled in 2020 in the midst of the global Covid-19 pandemic, Graham Norton returns once more to introduce live coverage from Rotterdam of the 65th Eurovision Song Contest. This year, the singer-songwriter James Newman will be representing Britain with his song Embers, co-written with Conor Blake, Danny Shah, Tom Hollings and Samuel Brennan. London-based Newman, who is 35, won the award for British Single of the Year at the 2014 Brit Awards for his song, Waiting All Night. Will he have what it takes to be crowned this year's winner?

programme.title: The Ring (2002)
programme.desc: A journalist investigates several teenage deaths, which are linked to a cursed videotape and the rumour that anyone who watches it will die a week later. Her growing obsession with the tape endangers herself and her family - and to avoid the predicted fate, she has just seven days to find out where the recording came from. Gore Verbinski's remake of the Japanese supernatural horror, starring Naomi Watts, Martin Henderson and Brian Cox

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Scotland look for a property in the Cotswolds on a budget of £600,000, and drops in on show jumping double Olympic gold medallist Nick Skelton

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott journey through Bedfordshire, where they learn the history of British airships and take the opportunity for Irita to drive a 120-year-old tractor

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the five remaining contestants are challenged to make a pair of identical gold hoop earrings, before creating a gold locket for a real-world client. Who will impress the judges enough to stay to make it through to the semi-final?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The inaugural Dagenham Sunday Market Food Awards take place, and two new traders show off their wares - edible cakes for dogs and wooden chainsaw sculptures

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having suffered flashbacks from her near-fatal experience with Stan O'Rourke, Sarah finds the strength to embrace her happiness with George

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter H, visiting the Hyacinth National Collection, before Monty Don demonstrates how to plant the bulbs. Plus, a look at keeping hens

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: How doing the laundry is harming marine life, why the fish-and-chip supper has changed and which lobster is making a comeback. With Steve Brown, Gillian Burke and Chris Packham

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Old College in Aberystwyth, Raj Bisram is intrigued by a 19th-century sewing box, and Christina Trevanion is wowed by a sapphire and diamond ring. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits a Regency restoration project at the National Botanic Garden of Wales

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From the Kent Cinque Port of Deal, Michael Portillo heads to Walmer Castle, home during the 1920s to Lord Beauchamp, whose openly homosexual lifestyle led to his exile from the country. And in Margate, Michael discovers the connection to TS Eliot

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift celebrate the positive power of plants as they focus on the designers and growers striving for a better world with the creations they bring to the event. They reveal how, over the years, the show has contributed to the better promotion of the health of the planet, plants and people, and talk to Countryfile presenter Ellie Harrison and BBC correspondent Fergal Keane about the importance of gardens to mental wellbeing

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Comedian Ed Gamble joins food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and cook and broadcaster Rachel Khoo to select the menu's final flourish - the dessert course. This is the last chance the chefs have of craft a course at the banquet celebrating British innovation, so tensions are running high as the rivals cook against each other for the final time. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis poke fun at recent news in the company of Catherine Bohart, Ed Byrne, Darren Harriott, Rhys James and Felicity Ward

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of Steve Davis and the rise of promoter Barry Hearn's Matchroom group of players, which brought lucrative sponsorships into the game. By the mid-1980s, snooker was at the peak of its powers and in 1985 nearly 20 million people tuned in to see Steve Davis play Dennis Taylor in the World Championship final

programme.title: The Psychedelic Drug Trial
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a ground-breaking new trial at Imperial College London, which sees a psychedelic drug tested against an antidepressant as a treatment for depression. Filmed over 16 months, the programme follows a team of scientists and psychotherapists as they compare the effects of psilocybin, the active ingredient of magic mushrooms, with an antidepressant on a small group of participants with clinical depression

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, beginning with the high Georgians. Lisa goes to Chatsworth House in Derbyshire in search of one of the beauty icons of the day, Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire, and discovers how in this period of extreme wealth in Britain, the rich entered an arms race of beauty. Later, she recreates an authentic Georgian look on a 21st century model

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A musical couple from London need Steve Brown's help to unearth a new home in Oxfordshire. On the £950,000 shopping list is a detached property with space for a studio and good links to the capital. Away from the search, Steve checks out a dairy farm where he samples kefir, purportedly an ancient, health-giving drink made from fermented milk

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that is hanging on the wall

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher takes on an antique toy cannon handed down to the owner by his grandfather, who bought it with his pocked money in 1907. It hasn't fired a shot for more than 50 years. Can Steve get it ready for combat once again? Instrument restorer Julyan Wallis takes on an 80-year-old banjo, Suzie Fletcher gets to work on a pair of 1930s roller skates and woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to bring an elaborate wooden salad bowl back to life

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: A look back at some of the biggest characters from the series, seeing whether the risk taking new traders are still in business, who has shuffled back to the day job, and who is making a killing? Plus, the five golden rules of trading are revealed. Narrated by Larry Lamb

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack has still not spoken to Carolyn, so Sarah decides she must do something to heal their wounds. Olivia returns from the Riviera with some unexpected news

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: As the end of the decade approaches, Sarah and George prepare to leave for his embassy posting in Israel, and Sarah tries one more time to reconcile Jack and Carolyn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: A look at whether herring, the 'silver darlings of the sea' are making a comeback. Plus, a man shares his passion for sea horses and Gillian Burke investigates whether being a brave crab is a disastrous strategy

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, while Paul Martin finds out about saddlemaking in Dudley

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Kent seaside resort of Herne Bay, where he learns about a pioneering aviatrix who began her working life as a typist in Hull. In the seaside town of Whitstable, he tucks into its famous staple, oysters. Michael later crosses the North Downs to Lenham, and visits Leeds Castle

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to James May, Gary Numan, David Olusoga, Rosie Jones and William Dalrymple to explore what people lose from arts and culture when they cannot travel. In London's St Pancras station, she asks if it is architecture and art that give train station reunions their romantic edge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver presents the series finale - an outdoor banquet at Jodrell Bank in Cheshire. The star-studded guests, including Professor Sarah Gilbert from Oxford's Covid vaccination programme. must vote for the overall winning chef

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds flowers to act as pollinators and creates an alpine trough. Adam Frost helps comedian Susan Calman with her garden, and Nick Bailey reveals a simple way to attract wildlife

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The pianist welcomes US musician Annie Clark, aka St Vincent. Plus, multi-talented actor and musician Johnny Flynn, Bristol-based five-piece Squid, and multi-instrumentalist and vocal powerhouse Laura Mvula

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Testament of Youth (2014)
programme.desc: When her fiance and brother are sent to the front line during the early days of the First World War, a young woman gives up her studies at Oxford University to follow them. She becomes a nurse, and the horrors she witnesses during the conflict change her outlook on life forever. Period drama based on the memoirs of writer Vera Brittain, starring Alicia Vikander, Kit Harington and Taron Egerton

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Kara struggles with the foreboding prediction of the Cylon Hybrid, while Adama and the Colonial fleet are in despair over their shocking discovery

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Admiral Adama prepares the fleet to leave Earth to find a habitable planet to colonise, but is forced to form an alliance with the Cylons when in need of their technology. However, members of the coalition refuse to accept the aliens as allies - causing an unlikely rebel partnership to form. Meanwhile, Gaius Baltar's gathering descends into a riot. Katee Sackhoff and James Callis star

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Marc, a public health worker who was diagnosed with HIV aged 17 in the late 1980s. He found support at the Landmark Centre in Brixton via a volunteer called John. Nearly 30 years later, Marc is desperate to thank John for helping him. Plus, Belfast dad Peter searches for a rehabilitation nurse called Betsy who helped him recover from a gunshot wound during the Troubles

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood uncover strange tales, including an unidentified black blob invading the California coast, and a lost underwater world halfway up a mountain

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A boy gets a den to remind him of his home town in Australia

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Evie and Alfie are joined by TV presenter Ben Cajee and comedians Zoe Lyons, Bec Hill and Marlon Davis

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A visit to a new theme-park area inspired by David Walliams' Gangsta Granny

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: The crew reaches the remote island of South Georgia, where Steve Backshall witnesses two elephant seals in battle as he walks with the largest colony of king penguins in the world. He's then given the opportunity to swim with the birds as they head offshore to feed, before travelling to his final destination - Antarctica. The naturalist is here with one mission - to dive with a leopard seal, one of the region's greatest predators

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines how it was that in a universe made of stars, rocks and endless space, a conscious civilisation was born. His latest adventure takes him from a submerged space station in Star City on the outskirts of Moscow, to Ethiopia, high above in the great Rift Valley, where he encounters the geladas, mankind's distant ancestors. Despite once being Africa's most successful primate, a species which at one time roamed across the entire continent, these days they are found in one just place in the remote Ethiopian Highlands. Cox investigates why these ancestors retreated, yet modern mankind has expanded across the planet

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Patrick Stewart narrates this documentary, following the last remaining members of the species as they fight for survival on the slopes of central Africa. Dominant silverback Cantsbee protects those close to him as they make a perilous descent to feed on bamboo. On the other side of the Rwandan volcanos, a young female gorilla looks to her father for guidance after being deserted by her mother, and a teenage silverback tries to strike out on his own in Uganda

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andi Oliver presents as the eight winning chefs from the regional heats battle to get their starter course to the banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. The rivals are cooking to impress Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton, Rachel Khoo and guest judge comedian Phil Wang

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight regional champions cook their fish courses as they battle to win a place on the menu of a banquet celebrating British innovation. Engineer and TV presenter Zoe Laughlin joins the judging panel of food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and food creative Rachel Khoo, to decide who should make it to the banquet

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This time, the eight regional champions cook their main courses in a bid to secure a place on the menu of a banquet celebrating British innovation. With the most complex dishes of the week to prepare, and only two spots left on the banquet menu, the chefs are pushed to their limits, and guest judge Carol Vorderman joins food critic Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Rachel Khoo as they pick the dish they want to see at the banquet

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on their visits to the Arctic, with Liz Bonnin looking back on her time helping a team of scientists locate a population of rare Siberian tigers. Gordon Buchanan recounting the time he went to Ellesmere Island in Canada in search of wild wolves that had never seen humans before, and Chris Packham remembers the time he ventured to Greenland on an expedition to uncover the secret life of an iceberg

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: It's the grand final, and the three finalists return to the workshop to compete to be crowned the winner. They are each challenged to make two pieces of truly outstanding jewellery - pair of pearl earrings and a bridal headpiece. Who will the judges decide is worthy of the title Britain's next big jewellery star?

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Not content with Jonesy's delivery van for safe and efficient transport, the bumbling platoon members decide to boost their defences and procure an armoured car. Classic comedy first aired in 1969, starring Clive Dunn, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society (2018)
programme.desc: In London, 1946, author Juliet Ashton's career has taken off but she is not enjoying promoting her detective stories to eager audiences on a book tour. When she receives a letter from a Guernsey pig farmer inviting her to attend a meeting of his literary society, she eagerly accepts. What Juliet discovers about her new friends and their secrets leads to her staying on the island much longer than she planned. Drama, starring Lily James, Tom Courtenay and Katherine Parkinson

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Poet Femi Nylander investigates the atrocities committed across the West African state of Niger by rogue French army captain Paul Voulet at the end of the 19th century. The officer's actions closely mirror the colonial horror depicted in Joseph Conrad's celebrated novel Heart Of Darkness, which was written at the same time that Voulet unleashed his killing spree in 1899

programme.title: Loving (2016)
programme.desc: In late-1950s Virginia, interracial couple Richard and Mildred Loving are targeted by the authorities and arrested, leading to a momentous legal battle that would end with the Supreme Court's historic 1967 decision. Director Jeff Nichols' critically acclaimed fact-based drama, starring an Oscar-nominated Ruth Negga, with Joel Edgerton, Will Dalton and Michael Shannon

programme.title: Margin Call (2011)
programme.desc: A bitter executive is dismissed from an investment bank, but plans his revenge by handing secret information on the company's fortunes to a junior analyst. When the information is decoded, it reveals that the company is on the brink of a devastating financial crisis, a revelation that is soon spreading throughout the workforce. Drama, starring Kevin Spacey, Paul Bettany and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob travels depicts a mountain ridge lake, a scene with powerful mountains, grassy meadows and little evergreens framing a shiny mere

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Sara Cox, Scott Mills and Chelcee Grimes monitor events in London, following the second semi-final in Rotterdam where a further 17 acts battle it out in a bid to secure a place in Saturday's final. UK viewers have the opportunity to vote for their favourite, while the UK entry, James Newman, joins the action from the Netherlands

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Docudrama portrait of Delia Derbyshire, the electronic sound pioneer behind the Doctor Who theme tune, exploring the idea that this extraordinary composer herself lived outside of time and space. Delia's story is told through two archives: the first, a collection of lost works, 267 reels of quarter-inch magnetic tape recordings of Delia's work found in her attic after her death, the other, her school books, paintings and keepsakes, discovered in her childhood bedroom

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob travels depicts a mountain ridge lake, a scene with powerful mountains, grassy meadows and little evergreens framing a shiny mere

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Science writer Steven Johnson and historian David Olusoga embark on an investigative journey through 300 years of scientific breakthroughs and go behind the scenes of modern medicine to meet the unsung heroes tackling Covid-19 and other public health threats in today's world. They begin by examining how the development of vaccines has helped to prevent the spread of serious disease and how new ones are developed when never-before-seen diseases emerge

programme.title: Sings Bacharach and David!
programme.desc: Archive performances of songs penned by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, featuring artists including Aretha Franklin, Dusty Springfield, the Carpenters, Sandie Shaw and the Stranglers. The programme also features Bacharach himself performing alongside Rufus Wainwright on Later with Jools Holland

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on January 3, featuring performances by artists including Betty Boo, Seal, Iron Maiden, Bill Medley and Jennifer Warnes, Black Box, C & C Music Factory featuring Freedom Williams, Anthrax, Patsy Cline and Paul Gascoigne with his Gazza Rap

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on January 10, featuring performances by artists including Bananarama, Pop Will Eat Itself, Robert Palmer, Whitney Houston, John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John, Off-Shore, Jesus Jones, MC Hammer and Enigma

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal narrates this compilation of all things Eurovision, on the evening of what would have been the global singing contest's 2020 event in Rotterdam. The alphabetical guide recalls some of the greatest moments in the show's 64-year history - as well as those perhaps best forgotten. Highlights include the best interval acts, comic voting gaffes and the funniest commentary moments from Terry Wogan and Graham Norton - not to mention dancing gorillas, Polish butter churners, fainting backing singers and Celine Dion on a tractor

programme.title: Eurovision at 60
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the song contest since its launch in 1956 up to 2015. The programme reveals how the annual event has blossomed from involving just seven countries to garnering some 195 million viewers across the continent. Hosts and competitors tell the behind-the-scenes story of Eurovision - widely regarded as the greatest and most eccentric song contest on Earth

programme.title: Abba at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of memorable performances by the group. Agnetha Faltskog, Bjorn Ulvaeus, Benny Andersson and Anni-Frid Lyngstad were thrust into the limelight in 1974 when Waterloo triumphed at the Eurovision Song Contest in Brighton, marking the start of their rise to fame. Various outings were captured by BBC cameras over the next few years and several are featured here alongside their Top of the Pops debut, archive interviews and footage featuring hits including Waterloo, Dancing Queen, Does Your Mother Know, Thank You for the Music, SOS, Fernando and Chiquitita. There is also a chance to see the foursome on a 1982 edition of Noel Edmonds' Late, Late Breakfast Show, which was Abba's last televised appearance in the UK

programme.title: Sings Bacharach and David!
programme.desc: Archive performances of songs penned by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, featuring artists including Aretha Franklin, Dusty Springfield, the Carpenters, Sandie Shaw and the Stranglers. programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto investigates the truth behind the sinking of Venice and discusses the likely long-term effect of a current exodus of inhabitants leaving to find jobs elsewhere. He explains how 19th-century British artists and writers, including Turner and Dickens, wove a romantic myth around the city, and describes his family's connection to one sexually promiscuous figure

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The explorer travels to the wilder reaches of south-eastern Europe, visiting Transdniester and meeting journalist Tatiana Tibuleac in Chisinau, the capital of Moldova. Crossing into the north of Romania, Michael marvels at the glories of the Byzantine painted churches of Moldovita and joins lumberjacks in the Vaser Valley

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: As police investigate the latest death, Louise struggles to accept what has happened. Peter is made an offer, but his son, Johannes, suspects he is up to something. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston explores cases that were cracked by examining the smallest traces of forensic evidence. They include the first murder case solved in the UK based on fingerprint evidence to the patterns of blood in a bedroom which helped overturn an infamous murder conviction. Gabriel also reveals the cutting-edge techniques that are proving vital to catch the killers of today

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer are puzzled by the reactions of their superior officers when their investigation leads in the direction of Hartmann's Liberal Party. programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 13-year-old Nigerian hyperrealism artist Kareem Waris as he stages his first exhibition in his hometown of Lagos. The film introduces Kareem and his family, before heading to the Ayowole Academy of Art, a makeshift art studio in a poor neighborhood in Lagos. From the picture he wants to draw of his grandma in a village many miles away, to the picture he wants to draw of Olu’s brother Samuel - a child amputee - through to the fishermen of the Makoko floating village and the elephants of Oyo National Park. These poignant pictures and their settings, hope to showcase the vibrancy, colour and beauty in Africa and evoke the wonder of Wakanda in Black Panther. After the pictures are created, it's on to the exhibition space, where many of Kareems peers and heroes await, in what promises to be an emotional and life-changing experience for Kareem and his family as he finally takes the first steps towards becoming a young master.

programme.desc: Would you turn your back on your religion just so you could fit in? 14 year-old Jag is a care-free, easy-going Sikh boy who loves nothing more than playing football with his mates, but he has finally lost his patience with his classmates after the new kid at school asks if he is wearing a handkerchief around his topknot. Jag feels like he spends his life answering stupid questions about his hair and has had enough. Is this something he is going to have to face all his life? And why didn't his friends stick up for him? Would life be simpler if he just cut his hair? After all his brother Kully did just that, and life seems easy for him now...but it's not quite that simple for Jag. In this short coming-of age-film, we see Jag make one of the toughest decisions of his life to date. Does he honour his Sikh family values and traditions and start to wear a turban, or will he cut off his hair to fit in with his mates?

programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are celebrating another noisy victory, gleefully ringing Big Ben's bell when an over-excited Penfold falls, pushing the clock hand forward, causing the sun to go down prematurely. Unbeknownst to them a mysterious figure watches on from nearby noting the clock's incredible time-controlling powers. When DM realises that the day is being stolen and replaced with a silent night, he enlists the help of expert colleagues - the night shift! programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

He solves a puzzle for Harry and Henry the Hedgehogs and encourages Brie the Bumblebee to get warm so she can fly. Brie is able to return the favour and points him in the right direction to meet Sid. But time is running out for Wilbur. Will Sid still be there when he arrives?

We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

After some more refusal from the Crystal Ball, Wizard Tripwick and Gail struggle to answer the questions themselves. Keith produces his own crystal football to help his team with the answers. After saying goodbye to the Crystal Ball and totting up the final scores, the winners of this GiggleQuiz are revealed.

This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books

Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel’s got some socks, a bib and a wooly hat inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different dressing-themed toys and objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide safe, baby-appropriate clothes or dressing-up toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Betty the Mouse’s little ones see a big shadow from their cosy nest in the potting shed, and all agree it must be a dinosaur. Word spreads around the garden and reaches Tilly the Tortoise, who is on a visit for the holidays. She can’t believe her luck – she loves dinosaurs! Tilly heads off on a search around the garden, bumping into Casey the Badger and Rosie the Rabbit, but no one has seen it. Auntie the Tawny Owl points her in the right direction – back to the potting shed! Will she ever find the dinosaur?

programme.title: Magic Hands

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of flowers to see how we can help to look after them and our planet. Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different. We discover the different parts of a flower, the stigma, the anther, the stamen and the petals and how some flowers go to sleep at night and open in the day!

programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway starts his walk at Porthkerry, next to his home town. On his way to Llantwit Major he drops in for tea at Font-y-Carlo caravan park and tries his hand at stone-stacking on a pebble beach. Comedian Kiri Pritchard McLean takes a nostalgic trip to Barry Lido, while Mo Jannah explores the stories of the African sailors who made south Wales their home

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Carters attend court, where a nervous Mick prepares to read his victim statement - unaware of the drama to unfold. programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Bobbie Edwards had to close her gym Colwyn Bay under lockdown, she launched women-only glutes boot camp courses instead and here reveals the secrets of its success. Bridgend-based entrepreneur Scott Magill reveals how he struck lockdown-gold with his doorstep doughnut delivery service

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Cherish Finden plus special guest Sarah Hadland. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip through Scotland, joining professional forager Gary Goldie at Airds Bay in Argyll and Bute to pick hogweed and sea arrowgrass to use as flavourings for a wild curry. She then heads to the Cairngorms National Park to visits the Blair Atholl watermill run by baker Rami Cohen, grinding her own flour to bake Indian five-spice soda bread, served with a red lentil dish. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a vegetarian haggis tarte Tatin

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: Swimming: European Championships
programme.desc: A round-up of the action so far at Danube Arena in Budapest, featuring British stars including Ben Proud, Molly Renshaw and Adam Peaty

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore's recycling mission takes her to Bredbury, Greater Manchester, where she finds a haul of pine doors, old rope and glass demijohns to convert into new, exciting objects

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Devizes, Wiltshire, each designing a wildlife-friendly garden that must also be ideal for everyday family life - on a budget of £1,500

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: Heavy snow closes the quarry, but does not deter the landlord's agent from making an impromptu inspection visit. In their third week of Victorian life, the families learn they may have few rights in their harsh new world, but they do at least have each other. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Blue Peter presenters from different eras of the long-running show.
