programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Alexandra Burke, as they assist two brothers from Middlesbrough in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a scientist, a prison officer and an electrician

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: After a year like no other, which saw the world-famous competition cancelled in 2020 in the midst of the global Covid-19 pandemic, Graham Norton returns once more to introduce live coverage from Rotterdam of the 65th Eurovision Song Contest. This year, the singer-songwriter James Newman will be representing Britain with his song Embers, co-written with Conor Blake, Danny Shah, Tom Hollings and Samuel Brennan. London-based Newman, who is 35, won the award for British Single of the Year at the 2014 Brit Awards for his song, Waiting All Night. Will he have what it takes to be crowned this year's winner?

programme.title: The Ring (2002)
programme.desc: A journalist investigates several teenage deaths, which are linked to a cursed videotape and the rumour that anyone who watches it will die a week later. Her growing obsession with the tape endangers herself and her family - and to avoid the predicted fate, she has just seven days to find out where the recording came from. Gore Verbinski's remake of the Japanese supernatural horror, starring Naomi Watts, Martin Henderson and Brian Cox

programme.title: Unbeatable

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester for three unwanted items he can save and sell on. Furniture restyler Chloe Kempster attempts to give an old tallboy a new lease of life, while woodworker Bruce Faulseit has an interesting challenge on his hands with a collection of slate tiles. For his own project, Jay has a trio of metal lights to overhaul

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott tour Somerset, acquainting themselves with public houses along the way, and Irita unexpectedly finds something to buy that's hanging on the wall

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rocky tells Sonia stories from her childhood, but when she remains unimpressed he goes on to regale the customers in the Vic with tales of his various escapades. Mick comes clean to Linda about his statement, an angry Martin confronts Zack, and Isaac's condition worries his family and friends

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Plant-able children's books are the offering from two friends hoping to gain a Dragon to grow their brand, and a pair of ex-bankers pitch their ambitious at-home private chef service. A Wirral-based entrepreneur pitches his range of luxury home fragrance products, while a husband-and-wife duo are keen to broaden out their scent-detection courses for dogs. Whether multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies will smell any potential profit remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: St Johnstone v Hibernian (Kick-off 2.00pm). All the action from the showpiece match at Hampden Park. Prior to Celtic lifting this trophy four years in a row, Hibernian were the last winners of the competition, beating Rangers in 2016 to win the competition for the first time since 1902. The Saints lifted the trophy for the first and only time in their history in 2014 when they overcame Dundee United and will go into this match with high hopes after a shock penalty shootout victory over the Gers in their semi-final

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, Angela Rippon, Julia Somerville and Gloria Hunniford dig into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of property developments in Plymouth, Treherbert in Wales and Hinckley in Leicestershire that all turn out to take much longer than planned, threatening to prevent their new owners making money

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a senior manager gambling with an NHS credit card, and an exotic dance troupe boss goes missing after defrauding the public purse of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A Scottish cyclist on a round the world challenge is hit by a driver in Texas, and a family dog saves the day when a woman slips and falls on the stairs. Nick Knowles presents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets shivers down his spine when he gets to find out more about some spooky antiques. Later, he and the contenders head to the auction in Derby

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Ed, Sheila, Ciara and Jas vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. It's an emotional brief in Cheddar, as the buyers want something uplifting for their home after a recent cancer diagnosis. With all three of the arty trio working in bold and bright colours, will it be an abstract artist, an intuitive painter or a stained-glass designer who brings smiles of joy to the homeowners?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton visits rural Shropshire with a couple who want their £500,000 budget to afford them and their young children a life in the outdoors with community close by. While in the county, Alistair visits the mansion of Attingham Park to see the recent restoration successes for himself, as well as discovering some intriguing stories from its past

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tim Medhurst and Irita Marriott head to the south coast of England for the final leg of their challenge, where Irina finds out about the history of beach huts while in Bournemouth, Dorset. Irita also fancies a table she knows Tim will hate, while Tim purchases a fine Chinese vase. However, the clock is ticking - the world's oldest clock at Salisbury Cathedral - which chimes for Tim

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on what happens when care goes wrong in hospitals, revealing how some NHS trusts seemingly keep critical reports hidden from the regulator and the public. Repeated scandals have shown how transparency is vital to make sure lessons are learned. However, after a year-long investigation uncovering around 100 unpublished independent reports, reporter and GP Faye Kirkland discovers how hospital failings are apparently being buried

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Carters attend court, where a nervous Mick prepares to read his victim statement - unaware of the drama to unfold. Sharon tells an unbelieving Zack he has to apologise to Martin, and as Tiffany sings Rocky's praises, Sonia remains unconvinced - until she is given food for thought from an unlikely source

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Richard Ayoade is the guest host, with broadcaster Richard Osman and peer Sayeeda Warsi joining regular team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the week's news headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Alan gets the chance to showcase the harder edge of his broadcasting chops by delivering an expose of the chemsex phenomenon and drama therapist Izzy Barnes leads a live workshop in the This Time studio. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Cherish Finden plus special guest Sarah Hadland. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip through Scotland, joining professional forager Gary Goldie at Airds Bay in Argyll and Bute to pick hogweed and sea arrowgrass to use as flavourings for a wild curry. She then heads to the Cairngorms National Park to visits the Blair Atholl watermill run by baker Rami Cohen, grinding her own flour to bake Indian five-spice soda bread, served with a red lentil dish. Back in her kitchen, Nadiya creates a vegetarian haggis tarte Tatin

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the final round of Premier League fixtures

programme.title: Swimming: European Championships
programme.desc: A round-up of the action so far at Danube Arena in Budapest, featuring British stars including Ben Proud, Molly Renshaw and Adam Peaty

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore's recycling mission takes her to Bredbury, Greater Manchester, where she finds a haul of pine doors, old rope and glass demijohns to convert into new, exciting objects

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers meet a couple in Clapham juggling older parents, twin babies and busy careers, while in Shrewsbury, a garden has nothing exciting for children

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Blue Peter presenters from different eras of the long-running show. Ayo Akinwolere, Mark Curry, Zoe Salmon, Simon Thomas, Mwaka Mudenda, Richie Driss, Katy Hill and Tim Vincent try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Alexandra Burke, as they assist two brothers from Middlesbrough in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a scientist, a prison officer and an electrician

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: After a year like no other, which saw the world-famous competition cancelled in 2020 in the midst of the global Covid-19 pandemic, Graham Norton returns once more to introduce live coverage from Rotterdam of the 65th Eurovision Song Contest. This year, the singer-songwriter James Newman will be representing Britain with his song Embers, co-written with Conor Blake, Danny Shah, Tom Hollings and Samuel Brennan. London-based Newman, who is 35, won the award for British Single of the Year at the 2014 Brit Awards for his song, Waiting All Night. Will he have what it takes to be crowned this year's winner?

programme.title: The Ring (2002)
programme.desc: A journalist investigates several teenage deaths, which are linked to a cursed videotape and the rumour that anyone who watches it will die a week later. Her growing obsession with the tape endangers herself and her family - and to avoid the predicted fate, she has just seven days to find out where the recording came from. Gore Verbinski's remake of the Japanese supernatural horror, starring Naomi Watts, Martin Henderson and Brian Cox

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Wednesday's business in the House of Lords including questions to government ministers and the final day of debate on the Queen's Speech focusing on defence and foreign affairs.

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Party historian Lord Lexden visits Astley Hall, home to the Conservative leader who was three times prime minister

programme.title: Wednesday in Parliament
programme.desc: The day's political proceedings, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Secretary Oliver Dowden and his team of ministers.

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: Air Iomall-Music from the Edge
programme.desc: Two of Scotland's leading young traditional musicians, Charlie Grey and Joseph Peach, take a musical voyage on the Dutch vessel Wylde Swan to some of the most remote islands off Scotland's north-west coast

programme.title: Churchill

programme.title: Dhan Uisge

programme.desc: Although Gibidh is quick to complain about others, a pleasant surprise awaits

programme.desc: Morag Ann MacNeil reads An Oidhche Mhìorbhaileach aig Bèibidh Broc by Karen Saunders, illustrated by Dubravka Kolanovic and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of Morris, a meerkat who lives in the Kalahari Desert with his parents and the rest of the meerkat gang. Mama is the leader and the only female who is allowed to breed. Dad is the top male, three years old and in his prime

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam

programme.desc: Catriona MacPhee and Donald MacSween introduce the best of the action from the Harelaw local trial in the tranquil setting of the Harelaw Fishery. Featuring David Kinloch and Nell, Jock Welsh and Cree and Stuart Davidson and Queen

programme.title: Le Sunnd a Seoladh
programme.desc: Profiling men from the Western Isles who served in the post-war merchant navy, focusing on their ties with New Zealand. Includes a mixture of archive footage, photographs and interviews that offer a glimpse into their experiences on board and in far-flung ports including New Zealand

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Lewis and Belfast in 2015 and 2016, featuring the likes of Dónal Clancy, Alasdair White, Seán Mac Corraidh and Paul Meehan

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the history of Highland place names in Assynt, and is joined by writer Finlay MacLeod to climb the 613m-high hill Stac Pollaidh. He also meets actor Kenny Macrae on Suilven - the area's equivalent of the Matterhorn

programme.title: Taigheadais Dùthchail (Rural Housing)
programme.desc: Ewen MacKinnon looks at thatched houses, and the intrinsic part they have played in the history of the Highland and Islands and its people. There are also a number variations of the blackhouse, and Ewen reveals how it has inspired new variations on this iconic Scottish house style

programme.title: Seirm

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe

programme.title: Tatu Rioghail Dhun Eideann (The Edinburgh Military Tattoo)

programme.title: An t-Inneal Espresso/The Espresso Machine

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes singers Tom Jones and Paloma Faith, Guardians of the Galaxy, Spectre and Blade Runner 2049 star Dave Bautista, comedian James Acaster and former boxer Nicola Adams. Plus, rock duo Royal Blood perform in the studio

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an all-star version of Name That Tune, with Shane Richie, Gabby Logan, Vick Hope and Richard Blackwood trying to identify songs played by a house band in the shortest time possible and have a chance at winning the £25,000 jackpot. Singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor makes a guest appearance at the piano

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Kelly Osbourne is among the guests joining Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz tests a ray that makes people dance. Magoo, wearing a snorkelling mask he's convinced is a virtual reality gaming headset, mistakes the ray for a weapon.

programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's challenge will see him attempt to escape from a helicopter simulator underwater. Has he got what it takes to be submerged?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The turtles turn to Mike for help when Aramis gets fed up and runs away. Can they convince her to come back?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Things are mysteriously disappearing from the Mikkelsen's house and Mike can't seem to get to the bottom of it. Could the raccoons have a new trick?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben gets a tough lesson on knowing his limits when he competes in a grueling obstacle race made even worse by the Weatherheads!

programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: TV presenter Vernon Kay and the UK's Eurovision entrant James Newman join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by Coronation Street star Simon Gregson and there are recipes for paella, lamb and Arbroath smokies, and there is a food adventure to L'Isle sur la Sorgue. Guest chefs Lisa Goodwin-Allen and Paul Foster also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have advice on baking, including a classic coffee and walnut cake, a traybake featuring fish, potatoes and croutons, and strawberry shortcake crunch

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James travels to Nottingham, where he sets up his mobile kitchen in the middle of Sherwood Forest and cooks a 'forest pork feast' for award-winning restaurateur Sat Bains

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a demolishment that causes even bigger problems, a wave attacking a holiday maker, and a giraffe getting up close and personal with an unwilling recipient

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Blackpool for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a special version of Strike It Lucky, with pairings from The Masked Singer, The Repair Shop and Love Island battling it out to win £25,000 for charity. Can they avoid the dreaded hotspots and come out on top?

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Mel B and Michelle Visage to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers. With a knife thrower, a saxophonist and even a globe-of-death rider in the mix, the teams will have their work cut out

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes actor and children's author David Walliams, actress and comedian Aisling Bea and singer and rapper Bree Runway, while singer-songwriter and former Oasis guitarist Noel Gallagher chats and performs in the studio

programme.title: Face/Off (1997)
programme.desc: FBI agent Sean Archer captures terrorist Castor Troy, the man who killed his son. While Troy is in a coma, Archer has the crook's face surgically grafted onto his own so he can go undercover and locate a bomb planted somewhere in Los Angeles. But then Troy regains consciousness - and in turn takes on Archer's identity. John Woo's action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Travolta, Joan Allen, Gina Gershon and Dominique Swain

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP is left holding the baby after upsetting the new childminder, but luckily Mike agrees to look after James at short notice. programme.desc: Greg realises Sally is attracted to him. Samantha agrees to move in with Des. Maxine tells Janice about Les's driving ban. Kevin negotiates the sale of the garage with Natalie

programme.desc: Graham tells Biff that he and Kathy kissed. Stella feigns illness to get away from Alan. Biff tells Kathy he can't go through with the wedding

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A woman seeks justice when her husband suffers a fatal heart attack after confronting a burglar. Meanwhile, Greengrass prepares for the gooseberry competition at the village fair

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is infuriated when consultant Charles Barber invites Mrs Price to a dinner. After a little digging she realises that she is the intended date

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George is told to mind his table manners when Mildred invites her sister round for a meal. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper discovers a cheap holiday scheme that requires the couple to join the Conservative Party - but George is a staunch Labour supporter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When Greengrass finds an unexploded bomb the Army is called in to deal with it, but one woman refuses to leave her home. Police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice tells Stella to let Alan down gently. Emily's dad returns home and tries to throw Butch out. Graham vows not to give up on Kathy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch is heartbroken over Emily. Frankie apologises to Zoe for Maggie's behaviour. Emily is frustrated with her dad

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les begs Janice to let him back in the house. Sally is furious about Kevin's deal for the garage with Natalie. Des begs Samantha not to get an abortion

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz urges Jim not to give in to hopelessness. Natalie tells Samantha she is sick for lying to Des. Zoe unburdens herself at her group therapy session

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The severed arm of a missing environmental health inspector is washed up on the shores of the Humber and Frost's investigation leads him to a local meat factory. Unfortunately, his new lodger's annoying dog keeps distracting him from the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Emma Kay, Niall O'Brien and Tom Georgeson

programme.desc: Natalie realises Samantha has duped her. Sally is flustered when Greg makes it clear he is interested in her. Nick and Leanne tell the police that Darren broke into their house

programme.desc: Kevin pushes Sally to sell her mother's house. Ashley prepares for Zoe's return home. Janice is upset at the realisation that she is now a single parent

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with excitement as the final preparations are made for the Doc and Louisa's big day. However, Martin is irritated by PC Penhale, who wrongly presumes he is best man - and to make matters worse, the bride is late. This isn't the first time the couple have tried to get married - will the villagers have to put the confetti back in the box again? Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star, with John Marquez, Eileen Atkins and Ian McNeice

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP is left holding the baby after upsetting the new childminder, but luckily Mike agrees to look after James at short notice. programme.desc: Richard infiltrates a mysterious agency in a risky bid to investigate the audacious assassination of several world leaders. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess takes up a new position, but feels constrained to consult Holmes about some of the bizarre conditions stipulated by her employer. Period crime drama, guest starring Natasha Richardson, Joss Ackland and Patience Collier, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When a deranged scientist creates a time machine, Steed and Mrs Peel show him the error of his ways. With Patrick Macnee, Diana Rigg, and guest star Peter Bowles

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A captured British spy is released when his Russian counterpart falls into British hands. C15 is tasked with ensuring his safety as well as establishing whether his information is genuine. However, such intentions fail miserably when he is poisoned. Bodie and Doyle set out to determine what part his daughter, a concert pianist, played in his death - assuming she really is his daughter

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's latest money-making idea involves a racing scam with an imaginary office. He hires a postal address and uses three public telephone boxes, and Terry has to make sure they are kept free. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Richard and Sharron take on bodyguard duties for a foreign president and his wife during a submarine trip - but the duo must keep their wits about them when an assassin finds their way on board. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt and Alexandra Bastedo

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by England international angler Matt Godfrey in the Cotswolds, where they target 5lb tench at an idyllic lake, before heading to the Gloucester canal late at night in search of a 10lb zander, otherwise known as 'the vampire fish'

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When the Ministry is broken into by an apparently invisible man, it seems a secret formula has fallen into the hands of an organisation run by Soviet Intelligence. Light-hearted action, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Members of the local travelling community are blamed when a car-dealer friend of Arthur's has the frighteners put on him. However, they soon prove useful allies when those actually responsible are revealed. Comedy drama, guest starring Jimmy Nail and June Whitfield

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by guest angler Gary Newman as they head to Croatia, where they target a rare 30lb grass carp. They also venture onto a wild river that will test their skills to the limit as they look to land a huge king carp

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenters Fiona Bruce, Rick Edwards, Kate Humble and Joe Swash pit their general knowledge against a trivia expert as they try to win thousands of pounds for charity. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a married couple that have made a booming business out of their hand-made Scotch eggs, and how a cotton mill-turned-soap factory is producing traditional bars, according to a process passed down through the generations. Plus, the team of craftsmen making the most accurate compasses in the world, and a behind-the-scenes look at the family firm making teddy bears that are cherished by children around the world

programme.title: The Blues Brothers (1980)
programme.desc: Two criminal brothers discover the orphanage where they were raised faces closure unless the nuns who run it can pay their tax bill, so the pair embark on an urgent mission to revive their old blues band to raise the money needed. Comedy, starring John Belushi, Dan Aykroyd, Aretha Franklin, James Brown, Ray Charles, Cab Calloway, Carrie Fisher, John Candy and John Lee Hooker

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An old adversary of Doyle's stirs up rivalry between factions of political terrorist group the Organization as a diversion in order to launch a revenge attack on the CI5 operative, who he blames for the death of his brother. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry fall under the spell of a bewitching Australian beauty who has ventured from Down Under in search of her missing fiance. However, she may just happen to be concealing an ulterior motive. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney visit three diverse venues across England. They first target barbel on the River Trent alongside Matt Godfrey, before switching their attention to carp on the Grand Union Canal, and finally teaming up with Tom Dove and Steve Ringer at Earlswood Lake

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Good Morning Britain's Ranvir Singh, journalist John Sergeant, ex-rugby international Ugo Monye and TV presenter Mariella Frostrup pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Richard and Sharron take on bodyguard duties for a foreign president and his wife during a submarine trip - but the duo must keep their wits about them when an assassin finds their way on board. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt and Alexandra Bastedo

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by England international angler Matt Godfrey in the Cotswolds, where they target 5lb tench at an idyllic lake, before heading to the Gloucester canal late at night in search of a 10lb zander, otherwise known as 'the vampire fish'

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When the Ministry is broken into by an apparently invisible man, it seems a secret formula has fallen into the hands of an organisation run by Soviet Intelligence. Light-hearted action, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A ministerial secretary innocently leaks information regarding a secret peace talks meeting to her lover, leading to a shooting incident and placing the future of the summit in jeopardy. While her boyfriend's involvement remains in doubt, Cowley gets her to pass on false information in the hope it will prove his innocence or confirm his guilt. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Members of the local travelling community are blamed when a car-dealer friend of Arthur's has the frighteners put on him. However, they soon prove useful allies when those actually responsible are revealed. Comedy drama, guest starring Jimmy Nail and June Whitfield

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by guest angler Gary Newman as they head to Croatia, where they target a rare 30lb grass carp. They also venture onto a wild river that will test their skills to the limit as they look to land a huge king carp

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenters Fiona Bruce, Rick Edwards, Kate Humble and Joe Swash pit their general knowledge against a trivia expert as they try to win thousands of pounds for charity. programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look at a married couple that have made a booming business out of their hand-made Scotch eggs, and how a cotton mill-turned-soap factory is producing traditional bars, according to a process passed down through the generations. Plus, the team of craftsmen making the most accurate compasses in the world, and a behind-the-scenes look at the family firm making teddy bears that are cherished by children around the world

programme.title: The Blues Brothers (1980)
programme.desc: Two criminal brothers discover the orphanage where they were raised faces closure unless the nuns who run it can pay their tax bill, so the pair embark on an urgent mission to revive their old blues band to raise the money needed. programme.desc: Dartmoor couple Maria and Andy met on Cyprus 20 years ago, got married there and now plan to live there permanently. They're particularly fond of Famagusta, and want a home that will work for them and their much-loved cat Keo. With their budget of £140,000, property expert Jean Johansson lines up five stunning homes for them to choose between

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Preston Hotel in Yeovil, owned by father and son Bis and Deep Oozageer. Ann and Rebecca find their room more cheap than cheerful, although an afternoon tasting the produce at a cider brandy distillery proves a massive hit

programme.desc: Amateur cook Filippo Adamo is keen to tantalise taste buds with his traditional creamy mushroom risotto. Chef Mark Sargeant aims to beat Filippo at his own game when they knock up some gnocchi. Andi Oliver hosts as the pair compete in the fast-paced cook-off

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets into the spirit of the season during a Christmas Eve session at Moe's, and after getting a tad too merry he is told not to come home by an angry Marge. Wandering around Springfield all on his own, the lovable oaf is left to think about his actions - but will he see the error of his ways?

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Flashbacks reveal new information about George's final moments. Darren decides to tell Nancy how he feels, and Cher continues to gaslight Mercedes

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Excitable French bulldog Buddy arrives at the centre. His owners have tried selling him seven times but every time he has been rejected. Can the staff finally find him a forever home? An eight-year-old girl who always wanted a small dog has her mind changed when she sees stray labrador Leah, and nervous collie Kimmie needs another dog to keep her company

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: In the series grand final, Charlotte Ritchie stumbles into some sand, Lee Mack dunks Little Alex Horne and Sarah Kendall stacks fish fingers. Meanwhile, Jamali Maddix staggers around a bit and Mike Wozniak staggers absolutely everyone - again - all in a bid to secure the most coveted prize in television - Greg Davies' golden head nestled on a spike

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: Comedy charting the highs and lows of Muslim punk band Lady Parts, as seen through the eyes of Amina Hussain - a geeky microbiology PhD student, who is recruited to be their unlikely lead guitarist at the instigation of fiery frontwoman Saira, who sees something in Amina that the others cannot. Amina is torn between two different worlds and on the lookout for love, romance - and a husband. Anjana Vasan, Sarah Kameela Impey, Juliette Motamed and Lucie Shorthouse star

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoughts on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: Tom Allen's Quizness
programme.desc: Contestants' general knowledge is put to the test as they play for the chance to win £25,000. However, the more answers the quizzers know, the dafter they must look to prove it. This first instalment features Dave, Hayley, Tharun and Saundra, who fight it out for a shot at the jackpot, facing tricky questions on anything from Captain Cook to Kelly Brook

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Pasadena, California, where he visits Jamaican-themed restaurant Cafe Kingston. The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: merican couples from different backgrounds temporarily switch spouses as they navigate emotive issues such as politics, class, gender and race with the ultimate goal of finding some common ground. In this episode, a professional wrestler swaps lives with a devoted wife and mother, whose disconnected husband prefers video games over family time

programme.title: How to Save a Grand in 24 Hours
programme.desc: Anna Richardson and the team give their expert financial, de-cluttering and DIY hacks to the Wallace family in Worcester, whose bank statements are not a pretty sight. They spend money faster than it comes in and mum Anna, who can't resist a bargain has a cosmetics drawer that rivals any professional make-up artist's. Dad Mark has fallen out of love with DIY and their youngest son refuses to eat the same evening family meal, which means hundreds of pounds is wasted on unnecessary cooking

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With two thirds of all British Covid deaths coming after September 2020, Dispatches investigates why, and examines what role the Prime Minister's decisions of last Autumn played in there being a massive second surge in infections that saw tens of thousands of people die. As Boris Johnson's former chief advisor Dominic Cummings threatens to reveal all, reporter Antony Barnett hears from some at the heart of Downing Street discussions, and learns why the Prime Minister ignored government scientific advice and continued to battle against another lockdown. Dispatches also probes the Prime Minister's border plans to stop virus variants coming into the UK and hears from a whistleblower who fears they've failed to do the job

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: A report from Yangon, the largest city in Myanmar, as young protestors risk everything to defy the military junta's coup. As the death toll in the city mounts, cameras witness the early optimism and hope of a protest movement disintegrate as the security services launch a brutal crackdown on unarmed civilians. The Myanmar government says it is responding to protests that harm the stability of the nation, using non-lethal force. However, as pressure to release political prisoners and journalists intensifies, so does the bloody crackdown

programme.title: The £100k Drop
programme.desc: Davina McCall presents the quiz show in which Andy and Mel from Stockport and Bristol couple Alice and Wallis start off with £100,000 and are challenged to place quantities of the cash over trapdoors as they face a series of questions, the wrong answers to which will lose them money every time they slip up

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lancashire couple Donna and Terry have been working together in their own jewellery business for the past 15 years and have decided that now is the time to spend less time at work and more time in the sunshine. Laura Hamilton is to find them a dream holiday home in Manilva on the Costa del Sol, so with their budget of £135,000, lines up five homes for the pair to consider

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners reunite, ready to air their grievances from throughout the week. Ann and Rebecca from Grange Farm Park aren't happy with Marilyn marking them down for the lack of a hot tub, and Marilyn herself isn't too pleased when her and Neil review the feedback for their slow breakfast service at Edgecliff Boutique B&B

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Culinary contender Angie Pinto hopes that her Caribbean-inspired vegetable curry will get executive chef Sophie Michell hot under the collar. Sophie, meanwhile, showcases her seafood fregola. Cookery challenge, hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge take the kids to Diz-Nee Land, where they soon become exasperated by the amusement park's lengthy queues, until they discover a ride that transports them to the home planet of Kang and Kodos

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: George's murderer prepares to come clean and reveal the truth. Elsewhere, Darren is disappointed to be stood up by Nancy, so Celeste offers to keep him company

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Violent hurricanes and severe drought have decimated once fertile areas of Central America, with the region's rural poor worst affected. Hunger and child malnourishment is widespread, prompting an exodus of migrants to the United States. Reporter Guillermo Galdos tracks the journey of Gonzalo, who desperate to give his family a better life, risks his own by employing a network of people smugglers to illegally enter the US. The film reveals how climate migrants have become a valuable commodity in a booming trade and in a region controlled by crime cartels, some are paying the ultimate price

programme.title: Tom Allen's Quizness
programme.desc: This edition of the comedy quiz sees Emma, Bernie, Gemma and Harv battle it out for a shot at the jackpot, facing tricky questions on anything from Christopher Columbus to Mr Tumnus. The twist is that if they want to win the £25,000, the quizzers will need to prove their brains by looking daft

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Katherine Ryan in the comedy words-and-numbers game, while Brett Domino joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: A Quiet Place (2018)
programme.desc: Two parents do whatever it takes to keep their children safe in a world full of creatures hunting every sound they hear. Nothing can be heard from the family hiding in silence, but all it takes is one noise and everything can go horrifically wrong. Horror, starring real-life husband-and-wife duo Emily Blunt and John Krasinski, who also directed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant which often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners' lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon Ramsay instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: National Heritage worker Gillian Sutherland hosts the first dinner party in Inverness, and has high hopes that her guests - emergency-call handler Melissa Kirby, property tycoon David Edes, hair stylist Zarina Ainslie and personal fitness trainer Jamie Tulloch - will appreciate her traditional Scottish menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Designer-label fan Melissa Kirby hosts the second dinner party in Inverness, hoping her cooking lessons from one of the city's best chefs will win her the £1,000 prize. However, rival Gillian is unimpressed when she finds out the chef also made the gravy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Property tycoon and teetotaller David Edes hosts a nautical evening for the third dinner party in Inverness, where his menu of 'basic but wholesome food' makes his guests wary. A night of suspicion and hilarity between the diners ensues

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the contest in Inverness, it is hairdresser Zarina Ainslie's turn to host. The fun-loving hopeful needs to live up to her lofty claims of being an amazing cook as her fellow contestants prepare to rate her on the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional footballer Jamie Tulloch hosts the final party in Inverness, going to the trouble of ensuring all the ingredients in his chosen dishes are locally sourced. However, it remains to be seen whether his home-grown approach to fine dining will be enough to impress his discerning fellow competitors, and as the night draws to a close, the week's winner is revealed

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert asks Ray and Debra to help with his criminal psychology course by taking IQ tests - but gives them misleading results to gauge their reactions

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra becomes jealous of a waitress who catches Ray's eye. Although the situation is perfectly innocent, the couple soon blow everything out of all proportion

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank has some anecdotes published in a magazine, sowing the seeds of grand writing ambitions. He approaches Ray's editor about becoming a columnist, but finds his way with words leaves a lot to be desired. Comedy, starring Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie decides 40 years of marriage to Frank is enough for any woman and moves in with Ray - to the delight of Robert, who enjoys being mothered by her. programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra's snooty parents announce they are going to pay a visit - much to the dismay of Ray, who pleads with Frank and Marie to be on their best behaviour and avoid embarrassing him. Ray Romano stars

Penny tries to support his ambitions, while Sheldon is jealous and attempts to discourage him from accepting the post. Meanwhile, Raja pushes the boundaries with Lucy, but puts their relationship in jeopardy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Penny bond as they miss Leonard, and Howard tries to help Raj get over his break-up with Lucy. Meanwhile, Bernadette and Amy visit an out-of-town biology convention. Comedy about four high-achieving scientists, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard returns from his research expedition in the North Sea and undergoes an adjustment period with Sheldon. Howard experiences an unusual emotional reaction as a result of his relationship with his mother. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes obsessed with a father-son relationship in a 1980s sitcom, using it as a source of inspiration to deal with Bart's hankering for a must-have mini-bike. However, when the youngster starts trading nuclear secrets with the Chinese government, Homer must find a new way to bring his son into line

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge notices grey hairs creeping through her blue dye and decides to embrace the shade like other middle-aged women. Homer becomes Moe's official 'wing man' in a bid to boost his friend's ailing love life

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Tension between Springfield Elementary's fourth and fifth graders spills over during a dodgeball game, leading Bart to arrange a fight with a fellow pupil. Marge gets back in touch with three friends she fell out with years ago, but she soon realises why they went their separate ways

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe achieves massive success when he turns the tavern into a gay bar, but keeps his hetrosexuality a secret to remain in his new clientele's favour. However, the decision backfires when the pub's new regulars encourage him to run for office and become Springfield's first openly gay councilman. Meanwhile, Principal Skinner falls for a new music teacher. Guest starring Kristen Wiig (Bridesmaids) and Alyson Hannigan (How I Met Your Mother)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Emma's faced with a very ill emergency patient, a baby nyala antelope. Meanwhile, Rachel introduce us to orphaned baby rhinos Ribbon and Lazuli

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to The Waverley Hotel in Callander in Scotland, owned by husband and wife Gordon and Jan Halladay. Ollie and Liz discover that the bathroom bin hasn't been emptied and best friends Tanya and Vicky get the gloves out for a thorough examination. Later, Jan challenges the group to become shipmates for the day and Tanya takes a shine to Liz. Over dinner, the group realise that Jan is the boss in her marriage. The next day the guests are looking forward to finally meeting Gordon, who's cooking breakfast. However, his confidence slips away when he serves over-cooked poached eggs, and Jan and Gordon are shocked and surprised by some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Greenview Guesthouse in Cumbria, owned by Tanya Briggs, with help from best friend Vicky Hope

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Hotel Sheraton in Blackpool, where the group are shocked by the sheer size of the hotel, run by mother and son Liz and Ollie Brown

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Katharine and Rory offer some proper Yorkshire hospitality at Chapter Two Accommodation in Malton, including an afternoon of sausage-making and a belly-busting breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Tanya defends the lack of breakfast offering at Greenview Guesthouse and is left flabbergasted when the payments are revealed

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: The launch of the site is just days away, but Johnny's last-minute design changes for his Maltese bus, including hand painting more than 200 tiles for the shower, are driving craftsman Pauleo to despair. Meanwhile, Bev is putting the finishing touches to the site up in Yorkshire before Johnny arrives to inspect the first of the vehicles, , including a converted American school bus and a beautifully restored Morris Minor Caravanette

La Rochelle are appearing in their first European final, while Toulouse, in contrast, are aiming for a record fifth title, but there has been little between these two sides in the Top 14 this season

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: In an episode dedicated to kitchen gardens and growing vegetables, herbs and fruit, the team come to the aid of two chefs in Wakefield who need a hand getting started at growing their own produce. Poppy Okotcha demonstrates which vegetables grow well in the same plots, Joel Bird plants a bee highway and creates a mushroom log, and Bruce Kenneth builds a bespoke greenhouse made from upcycled stained glass. Plus, comedian Tom Allen gives a tour of his own garden space

programme.title: Formula 1

programme.desc: Documentary exploring the murder of five-year-old April Jones in 2012, featuring previously unheard tapes of the 18 hours of interviews the police conducted with killer Mark Bridger, who was arrested within 24 hours of April being reported missing and charged four days later. The programme also features contributions from forensic psychologist Dr Joe Sullivan and April's family, who share their distress at her body remaining undiscovered

programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb's investigation into the notorious 17th-century witch trials concludes with a profile of Matthew Hopkins, the opportunistic puritan witch-hunter who transformed paranoia about Satanism into a lucrative business as he hanged hundreds of people for crimes they could not possibly have committed. Making use of contemporary reports, Suzannah paints a portrait of a man who believed he could cross any boundary and break any law to achieve his goals. Naming himself 'Witchfinder General', Hopkins toured the country charging fees for his services, but Suzannah's research reveals that ultimately, the public's distaste for witches was not as strong as its hatred of being conned by charlatans. Hopkins would eventually die of tuberculosis in relative obscurity, but his malign legacy lived on across the Atlantic, as witch-hunts began in Salem, Massachusetts

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras focus on the island of Taiwan, and three extraordinary railways. The journey begins in the eastern port city of Hualien, from where we travel north along the rugged Pacific coast. The trek ends with a view of Taiwan's highest mountain peaks above a sea of clouds. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Revisiting the musical highs of 1991 in the company of Shaun Ryder, Jayne Middlemiss, Paul Gambaccini and others. It was the year KLF dominated the charts, along with Vic Reeves, Hale & Pace, and Chesney Hawkes. The latter discusses his track The One and Only, and Beverley Craven looks back on the success of her romantic ballad Promise Me

programme.title: The Story of the Songs
programme.desc: The stories behind three of the songs that cemented singer and songwriter Rod Stewart as a musical superstar looking at Sailing, Do Ya Think I'm Sexy? and Young Turks

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Nick Wallis examines how CCTV means wrongdoers can no longer hide in a crowd, looking at its use in convicting people involved in the August 2011 riots across England

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels to the coastal forests of southern Sweden to meet 24-year-old single mother Annalisa, a native Italian who left everything in her home country behind when she was a teenager, and is now developing a forest hideaway on the fringes of society for her and her toddler. Ben helps her chop wood and make her home baby-proof, while Annalisa goes busking to make a small living. They also go dumpster-diving in search of rubbish that can be put to good use to counter the amount of waste thrown away

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: The stories behind some of the most dramatic escape attempts from the tower, including Henry Howard, who tried to avoid execution by making his way through the sewage system. In the present day, curator Sally Dixon-Smith deciphers some graffiti by a Tudor mystic who mysteriously disappeared, while Yeoman warder Chris Clawson reveals why the tower is still a spooky place to work in after dark

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski visits Liverpool's two cathedrals, which lie at either end of Hope Street. Despite having been built within 60 years of each other they represent starkly contrasting styles, the Anglican edifice being a vast tribute to neo-Gothic design and its Catholic counterpart a typical example of 1960s modernism

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice visits Essex to offer advice to a Colchester couple unable to snap up their dream home until they can sell their current house. Unfortunately, after four months on the market, there is still no buyer for the three-bedroom detached property - which comes complete with a wild colour scheme

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Part one of two. Rachel and a heavily pregnant Phoebe stay at home while Chandler, Joey and Monica go to England for Ross's wedding to Emily. Once there, however, it seems all Joey and Chandler can do is bicker. Guest starring Richard Branson and Sarah Ferguson

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Part two of two. As everyone makes their final preparations for Ross's wedding in London, Rachel tries to get a flight out to declare her love for him before he marries Emily. Guest starring Jennifer Saunders, Elliott Gould, Hugh Laurie and Tom Conti

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Despite Ross's massive blunder, he and Emily go through with the wedding - but she is clearly devastated and sneaks out of a window at the reception, declaring she never wants to see him again. Guest starring Helen Baxendale, Elliott Gould and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel returns from Greece and Chandler proves popular with the womenfolk. Phoebe feels upset about missing out on all the excitement in London, so the gang decides to treat her to a weekend in Atlantic City. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Matthew Perry

programme.title: The Bridge at Remagen (1969)
programme.desc: As Allied forces prepare for a major assault on Germany, a small unit of American soldiers is sent to capture a strategically important bridge over the Rhine - but German forces are determined to prevent the Allied advance, and seek to destroy it. Second World War adventure, starring George Segal, Robert Vaughn, Ben Gazzara and Bradford Dillman

programme.title: The Glenn Miller Story (1954)
programme.desc: Musical biopic of the bandleader and trombonist. The film depicts the king of swing's triumphs from his college days to the pinnacle of his career, before it ended when his plane mysteriously disappeared over the English Channel in 1944. Starring James Stewart, June Allyson and Charles Drake, and featuring an appearance by Louis Armstrong as himself

programme.desc: Musical biopic of the bandleader and trombonist. programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer concludes her journey around the Caribbean, visiting Grand Cayman for a spot of horse-riding along the beach. She then heads to Roatan off the coast of Honduras, discovering the wonders of the deep aboard a submarine, before finishing her trip on the Mexican island of Cozumel, where she parties the night away at the colourful local carnival

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The Owen family try their hand at self-sufficiency with their very first dairy cow, and Reuben accompanies his mum and younger sisters to pick up Buttercup from a neighbouring farm. Back at the farm, the family take on the toughest job ahead of the imminent breeding season, rounding up the free-roaming ewes who have spread themselves across hundreds of acres of wild moorland

programme.title: Harry & Meghan: 3 Troubled Years
programme.desc: A look at the Duke and Duchess of Sussex's relationship with the royal family as they embark on their new life in America, reflecting on what really went wrong and asking if it can ever be repaired. With the couple being offered new opportunities including lucrative book deals and TV opportunities, the film examines whether the Sussexes can manage to make a go of it outside the 'firm'

programme.title: The Queen Mother
programme.desc: Documentary examining the life of one Elizabeth Angela Marguerite Bowes-Lyon, who married George VI and was later known as the Queen Mother when her daughter Elizabeth ascended the throne. A combination of archive material and interviews with historians, royal insiders and journalists is used to tell the story of how an aristocratic debutante transformed herself - under the shadows of abdication and the Second World War - into the Queen Consort

programme.title: Live Boxing
programme.desc: Sam Eggington v Carlos Molina. Coverage of the bout for the vacant WBC Silver Middleweight title, held at the Skydome in Coventry. Eggington bounced back from a shock points defeat at the hands of Ted Cheeseman with an impressive sixth-round stoppage of Ashley Theophane in his last contest five months ago. However, he now faces a tough test against the former IBF Light Middleweight champion, who has won his last eight fights

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle travels to Brazil to meet Chris, a Scotsman who has chosen to make his home in one of the most remote areas of the jungle near the Amazon river. Ben discovers how Chris's love affair with the rainforest began when he had a near-death experience, and how he fought for almost three decades to protect this part of the world. Ben also has an encounter with a tarantula, and gets stuck into a battle against termites in the heat and humidity. The duo also go hunting for wild pig and fishing for piranha

programme.title: Peppa Pig
